@DarkEndMoon1 @mrddmia @RonColeman I'm not saying that wouldn't be a good rule to have. I am saying that the claim that it's obvious that it's the case seems quite absurd without some citation to some authority holding so.

@DarkEndMoon1 @mrddmia @RonColeman It is not obvious to me that given that Congress has the power to create or destroy courts inferior to the Supreme Court, they nevertheless don't have the power to monitor with little or no restriction what goes on in those inferior courts.

@dogg_not @RAHarrisonPA @RobertFreundLaw @DrSarteschi Also, to address the libel issue more directly, we don't hold people liable for libel absent some evidence of wrongdoing. If, with no wrongdoing, I libel you and cause you severe damages, you still have no remedy at law in the United States.

Why do we do this? Because we don't

probability.last_reasoning: Bear case increased from 10% to 12%: Extreme Fear at 14, XRP at $1.36 (significant drawdown), compound stress deteriorating with USD/JPY breaching elevated, trade war expanding, and Hormuz escalation risk elevated. Base case slightly increased to 42%: the regulatory acceleration (Trump demanding legislation, CFTC guidance imminent) and Ripple product consolidation strengthen the institutional infrastructure story, but macro headwinds and price action create a gravitational pull toward the lower range of base outcomes. Mid case increased to 35% from 38%: JoelKatz signals and regulatory momentum support this range, but macro stress offsets — net slightly lower as near-term execution risk rises. Bull case trimmed to 11% from 12%: macro deterioration makes explosive upside less probable in current environment. Net shift: slight reallocation from extremes toward base case, reflecting improved regulatory clarity against deteriorating macro backdrop.

bear_case.score_reasoning: Counter-thesis pressure is moderate-high. XRP price at $1.36 in Extreme Fear territory demonstrates the market is not pricing institutional adoption. Three critical kill switches lack data (ODL, ETF AUM, permissioned DEX), meaning the thesis is flying partially blind. The RLUSD recovery is positive but on-chain XRPL supply of only $345M vs $1.584B total means the stablecoin is primarily an Ethereum product, not an XRPL-native one. Macro deterioration is accelerating across multiple vectors simultaneously.

competing_infrastructure.name: SWIFT GPI
competing_infrastructure.status_text: SWIFT continues incremental upgrades; no breakout capability announced but 11,500-bank network effect remains the primary competitive moat against all challengers including XRPL.

competing_infrastructure.name: Visa B2B Connect
competing_infrastructure.status_text: No significant competitive announcements this cycle; Visa B2B Connect remains operational but has not demonstrated settlement layer ambitions that directly threaten XRP corridors.

competing_infrastructure.name: JPMorgan Kinexys
competing_infrastructure.status_text: JPMorgan's institutional blockchain payments network continues expanding quietly; bank-native infrastructure could capture same institutional corridors Ripple is targeting without requiring XRP as a bridge asset.

competing_infrastructure.name: BIS Project Nexus
competing_infrastructure.status_text: BIS multi-CBDC infrastructure remains the most credible sovereign-backed alternative to private bridge assets; if Nexus demonstrates independent operation without XRPL integration, it directly undermines the settlement layer thesis.

competing_infrastructure.name: Ethereum Institutional
competing_infrastructure.status_text: RLUSD's own data tells the story — 78% of RLUSD supply ($1.24B) lives on Ethereum vs $345M on XRPL. Ondo Finance tokenized stocks inherit NYSE/Nasdaq liquidity on Ethereum-adjacent chains. Institutional RWA tokenization is gravitating toward Ethereum infrastructure, not XRPL.

bear_case.odl_stagnation: ODL volume data has been unavailable for an extended period; Ripple's rebranding to unified 'Ripple Payments' may permanently obscure standalone ODL metrics, making the $25B kill switch potentially unfalsifiable — which is itself a bear signal.
bear_case.token_velocity_concern: No token velocity or average hold time data available. If XRP is primarily used as a momentary bridge (high velocity, low holding), price appreciation from utility alone is structurally limited regardless of volume growth.

macro_headwinds.name: Global Recession Risk
macro_headwinds.status_text: Trade war expanding (Spain, China), Extreme Fear at 14, DXY collapsing to 99.07, S&P under pressure at 6,816 — multiple recession indicators flashing simultaneously.

macro_headwinds.name: Crypto Winter Signals
macro_headwinds.status_text: XRP down to $1.36, Fear & Greed at 14 (Extreme Fear), but institutional infrastructure buildout (ETFs, regulatory clarity) argues against full crypto winter — more likely a macro-driven correction than structural capitulation.

macro_headwinds.name: Regulatory Reversal Risk
macro_headwinds.status_text: Trump's direct advocacy for crypto legislation, CFTC guidance incoming, and bipartisan Congressional engagement make regulatory reversal the lowest-probability bear scenario currently.

macro_headwinds.name: Rate Hike Extension
macro_headwinds.status_text: US 10Y at 4.05% with DXY at 99.07 creates an unusual divergence — yields steady while dollar weakens. If inflation resurges from tariffs and oil, Fed could extend restrictive stance, pressuring risk assets including crypto.

bear_case.bear_narrative: HOLD with increased monitoring. Bear pressure at 38/100 — the thesis is under structural pressure from stablecoin-native settlement (the dominant threat at 8/10, confirmed by Ripple's own behavior) but is not failing: no kill switches triggered, institutional commitments continue accumulating, and regulatory environment is the most favorable in XRP history. The central paradox remains unresolved: Ripple's product language is moving away from XRP while Ripple's $40B treasury demands XRP remain essential — and we cannot determine which force wins without technical documentation that doesn't exist publicly. Two kill switches are at WARNING (ODL Volume, RLUSD Circulation) and compound stress is pre-loaded with all three legs deteriorating. What I don't know — whether XRP plays any role in Ripple Payments' settlement backend — is the single question that most determines whether this thesis survives the stablecoin transition or is gradually hollowed out by it.
